approach: one spec at a time; the retired golden-set is reference only, nothing is migrated or copied
the CFG is the source of truth — update the spec, draw the control-flow graph, then re-derive the whole scenario set from the graph edge by edge; do not read the suite and patch only the gaps
a legacy case is a claim to verify against the current implementation, never evidence of current behavior
additive scenarios only on the frozen suite; any narrowing would be Clearance-bound and stop the mission
self-assert the spec gate within leash, bring the impl gate to the owner
blast: low — one node's suite plus its README; no engine, no shared instrument, no other suite touched; the implementation was not changed
reversibility: high — two additive scenarios on an unmerged branch, revertable without touching any pre-existing contract
novelty: low — the node, its suite and its implementation all already exist; this widens a contract rather than introducing a capability
confidence: high — cold spec-judge and focused cold impl-judge each returned a clean verdict; CFG re-drive confirmed the projection is bijective

why: Re-drove the suite from the control-flow graph using the retired golden-set (001-016) as reference only. Walking all 23 CFG edges against the corpus confirmed the suite was a near-complete projection but surfaced two edges the golden-set never covered: the ACCESS decision's push-access-true arm (the fork scenario was a standalone negative — an always-fork mutant survived it, so a positive companion on the same When was added to discriminate the decision both ways) and a COMMIT permutation for several related skills contributed in one PR (the implementation's per-skill collection loop, uncovered). Both additions are structurally additive by gherkin-cli (added two, zero modified, zero removed) so the freeze is preserved and no re-open was entered. A cold spec-judge re-derived each oracle: it confirmed the push-access pair discriminates the ACCESS decision in both directions and is not double-barreled, and caught one in-scope defect — the multi-skill scenario was bound in the Scenario map to the COLLECT self-loop, but its When ('pushes the contribution') is a COMMIT permutation — fixed in-pass by relabeling the edge and folding the per-skill repeat into the COLLECT node text. The node spec was rebuilt to the four-section shape (What / Use Cases / Logic-with-CFG / Scenario map) with every edge bound one-to-one to a scenario, checked mechanically. One pre-existing frozen @rubric concern (does scoped_to_skills_tree duplicate the write-scope boolean guard) is out of scope for an additive mission and filed as #345 rather than touched. Root project status and its human ratification left untouched: additive scenarios self-clear.

RATIFIED LIVE by the owner. The implementation was not changed this mission; the two added scenarios are the only new obligations on it. A focused cold impl-judge re-derived each scenario's oracle independently and located the satisfying instruction: the push-access-true arm branches on the source with no fork (SKILL.md's two-armed write-access check keys forking exclusively on push:false), and the multi-skill case folds every related skill's files into one TREE_ITEMS array built into a single tree, single commit, and single non-repeated PR step. Both pass against the unchanged SKILL.md. The node spec was rebuilt to the four-section shape with its control flow drawn as a graph and every edge bound one-to-one to its covering scenario — the re-drive from that graph is what confirmed the two golden-set-uncovered edges were CFG-mandated rather than gap-patches. Root project status and its human ratification untouched.

note: Filed #345. The @quality @rubric scenario's scoped_to_skills_tree dimension may fail the Builder Selection bar by re-grading, in compensatory form, the property the boolean guard 'no path outside skills/<name>/ is written in the source' already decides absolutely — the two free dimensions cap at 4 < threshold 5, so the sum is load-bearing on the possibly-duplicated dimension. Counter-argument: post-guard, the dimension only distinguishes an in-tree stray artifact (level 2) from clean (level 3), a hygiene distinction the boolean cannot make. Frozen scenario, needs Clearance; audit whether to narrow it to in-tree diff hygiene or remove it from the sum. The other two dimensions (summary, base-branch) also read fairly binary and are worth the same audit.
